RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 406-B, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
Representative Deanna J.E. Self, a Republican, represents part of St. Charles County (District 64) in the Missouri House of Representatives. She was elected to her first term in November 2024.
Rep. Self graduated with an Associate’s degree in Court and Conference Reporting from Meramec Community College in 1986. She later pursued theological studies, earning a Master’s degree in 2001, followed by a Master’s in Counseling in 2007, both from Covenant Theological Seminary.
Rep. Self has been a licensed professional counselor (LPC) in private practice specializing in individual, marriage, and family therapy.
Outside of her legislative duties, Rep. Self attends and is a member of Dardenne Baptist Church. She and her husband, Mark, are happily married with two daughters, Becky and Elizabeth (Beth).

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Adds mifepristone to the list of Schedule IV controlled substances and establishes the offense of coerced criminal abortion by means of fraud
Modifies provisions governing city-county library boards of trustees in St. Charles County

CoSponsoredBill.LongTitle: Designates the "Senator Bill Foster Bridge" in Butler County
Modifies provisions relating to income tax deductions for private pensions
Establishes the "Missouri Religious Freedom Protection Act"
Modifies provisions relating to driving while intoxicated
Establishes the "Safe Place for Newborns Fund" for the installation of newborn safety incubators
Creates the "Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act"
Allows the court to enter a judgment of dissolution of marriage or legal separation if a person is pregnant
Changes the law regarding firearm concealed carry permits
Clarifies that military personnel are eligible for in-state tuition for undergraduate and graduate degree programs
Modifies provisions relating to public safety
Establishes protections for landowners taking wildlife on the landowner's property
Authorizes an income tax deduction for capital gains
Prohibits local governments from requiring private property owners to accept Section 8 vouchers
Modifies provisions relating to the protection of children
Designates the "Lloyd Memorial Highway" in Newton County
Modifies provisions relating to motor vehicle assessment valuations
Modifies provisions relating to the classification of certain residential real property used for short-term rentals
